author: nflaig
content: we do have a queue for payloads implemented and also publish them slightly later right now because I suspected already that this would become an interop issue, this might also be why we didn't see any issues with Lighthouse<>Lodestar

author: potuz
content: Yeah, I rather not add artificial timings, IRL we may see payloads before blocks in large colocated clients.

author: nflaig
content: it's not even artifical, we wait currently to receive response from publishblock before we call publishpayload, which is simpler to implement, but not ideal of course, intending to do that in parallel later

author: potuz
content: ah makes sense. Perhaps even in that situation you may receive payloads before if the payload is empty and the block is not

author: nflaig
content: yeah it only ensures this doesn't happen on the api layer because we don't have a queue there, but on gossip we queue payloads if we recieve them earlier, same will be done for data columns later
